title: Mari'ha Andona
content:
{{Top|ga|leg}} {{Otheruses|Mari}} {{Character |type= |image= |name=Mari'ha Andona |homeworld= |birth= |death= |species= |gender= |pronouns=She/her{{StoryCite|story=The Last One Standing: The Tale of Boba Fett|book=Tales of the Bounty Hunters}} |height= |mass= |hair= |feathers= |eyes= |skin= |cyber= |families= |parents= |partners= |siblings= |children= |affiliation= |masters= |apprentices= }} {{Quote|Which part of this are you having difficulty with, Control?''"
"''I'm going to need a couple minutes. The flight droid doesn't like you.|A call between Han Solo and Mari'ha Andona|}} '''Mari'ha Andona''' worked as a [[Flight controller/Legends|flight controller]] for a sector on the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Coruscant/Legends|Coruscant]]. In [[19 ABY/Legends|19 ABY]], the [[New Republic/Legends|New Republic]] [[General/Legends|general]] [[Han Solo/Legends|Han Solo]]—an old friend of Andona's who was experiencing nostalgia for his prior [[Smuggler/Legends|smuggling]] career—contacted flight control to request a flight plan. Admitting to Andona he did not have a destination in mind at the time, Solo nevertheless requested she push through clearance for him to depart Coruscant. Despite predicting an upset reaction from her [[Unidentified flight droid|flight droid]] colleague and being berated later by her [[Mari'ha Andona's supervisor|supervisor]], Andona still provided Solo with a flight plan, with the general leaving the planet afterward. ==Biography== {{Quote|I'm going to take the ''Falcon'' up for a bit. Any chance I could get you to pipe me a flight path?''"
"''Yes, sir. What's your destination?''"
"''Haven't got one.''"
"''Excuse me? Sir?''"
"''I don't have one. I don't know where I'm going yet.|A call between Han Solo and Mari'ha Andona|}} [[File:HanSoloJubilar-TERC.jpg|thumb|right|250px|Han Solo (pictured) was an old friend of Mari'ha Andona who sometimes made irregular requests of her.]] Mari'ha Andona was a [[Flight controller/Legends|flight controller]] for a sector on [[Coruscant/Legends|Coruscant]], the [[Core Worlds/Legends|Core Worlds]] [[Ecumenopolis/Legends|city-world]] that served as the [[Capital/Legends|capital]] of the [[New Republic/Legends|New Republic]],''[[The Essential Atlas]]'' who served by [[19 ABY/Legends|19 ABY]].''[[The Essential Reader's Companion]]'' dates the main events of "[[The Last One Standing: The Tale of Boba Fett]]," in which Mari'ha Andona appears, to [[19 ABY/Legends|19 ABY]]. She worked in a flight control tower using a [[Computer/Legends|console]] that was equipped with a [[Comm unit/Legends|comm unit]], a [[Hologram/Legends|hologram]] viewing area to her right side, and a number of screens that displayed all of the flights in her sector—which was such a large quantity that it was hard for a [[Human/Legends|Human]] to pick out any individual marker of a flight. Andona worked alongside a [[Unidentified flight droid|flight droid]]. By 19 [[Galactic Standard Calendar|ABY]], Andona had been doing her job for long enough that she was aware of a tendency by [[Han Solo/Legends|Han Solo]], a Human New Republic [[General/Legends|general]] and former [[Smuggler/Legends|smuggler]] who was also an old friend of hers,{{CSWECite|I|30|Andona, Mari'ha}} to only use his [[Military rank/Legends|rank]] when he was going to be forceful when communicating with flight control personnel. At one point that [[Standard year|year]], Solo—who was feeling nostalgic for his smuggling career—decided to go on an improvised trip away from Coruscant aboard his [[YT-1300 light freighter/Legends|light freighter]],{{CSWECite|II|313-314|''Millennium Falcon''}} the [[Millennium Falcon/Legends|''Millennium Falcon'']]. He hailed flight control, with Andona answering the call and grimacing at Solo's self-addressing as general. Solo admitted that he did not yet have a destination in mind, which made the flight controller sigh in anticipation of the [[Flight droid/Legends|flight droid]] reacting negatively to the general's unscheduled departure. When Andona let Solo know that his request for a flight path would require several [[Standard minute/Legends|minutes]] to process, the general demanded that she hurry up, jokingly threatening Andona with the prospect of him personally visiting the flight control tower and charming her "to [[Death/Legends|death]]." The flight controller subsequently submitted Solo's request for clearance and then repeatedly entered a command to override the flight [[Droid/Legends|droid's]] objections. Finally, Andona cleared Solo for departure, and, after an exchange of sarcastic remarks, the general went on to leave Coruscant, ultimately traveling to the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Jubilar/Legends|Jubilar]] in the [[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outer Rim Territories]].''[[The Essential Atlas]]'' — Based on corresponding data for [[Jubilar system/Legends|Jubilar system]] Shortly after Solo's call, Andona's [[Mari'ha Andona's supervisor|supervisor]] contacted the flight controller via a holocall. Having learned of the general's departure, the supervisor berated Andona and questioned her for any information on Solo's destination or [[Time/Legends|time]] of return. In response, the flight controller could only tell that Solo had entered [[Hyperspace/Legends|hyperspace]] approximately twenty minutes prior. ==Personality and traits== {{Quote|You ''need'' to clear a corridor and give me a flight path and do it right now before I have to go down to the tower personally and ''charm'' you to death. Do you copy ''that''?''"
"''I copy you, General. And … here you go. Have a nice trip, General. Don't hurry back.''"
"''Try not to miss me too much, sweetheart. A pleasure as usual. Solo out.|A call between Han Solo and Mari'ha Andona|}} Mari'ha Andona was an experienced flight controller. She could be counted on to bend the rules for Han Solo and to approve his occasionally-unorthodox flight plans; however, she could still be taken by surprise by such irregular requests—though not to the point where she would lose her composure. Nevertheless, she was annoyed by Solo's use of his military rank to achieve what he wanted, and she let her annoyment seep into a conversation she was having with the general when she predicted the negative reaction from her flight droid colleague to Solo's unorthodox request. In that same exchange with Solo, Andona also expressed a sarcastic streak. ==Behind the scenes== Mari'ha Andona first appeared in "[[The Last One Standing: The Tale of Boba Fett]]," a short story by [[Daniel Keys Moran]] that was published in the [[1996]] anthology ''[[Tales of the Bounty Hunters]]''. ==Appearances== {{Indexpage}} *{{StoryCite|story=The Last One Standing: The Tale of Boba Fett|book=Tales of the Bounty Hunters}} {{1st}} ==Sources== *''[[Star Wars Encyclopedia]]'' *''[[A Guide to the Star Wars Universe, Third Edition, Revised and Expanded]]'' *''[[The Complete Star Wars Encyclopedia]]'' ==Notes and references== {{Reflist}} {{Interlang |de=Mari'ha Andona }} {{DEFAULTSORT:Andona, Mari'ha}} [[Category:Flight controllers]] [[Category:Individuals of unspecified gender]] [[Category:Individuals of unspecified species]] [[Category:Individuals with she/her pronouns]]
